content.content:
1→(* FVI (Find Vector Intersection) pure geometry functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d1/fvi.cpp. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effects for on-demand data fetching. OCaml FVI fetches segment/object
     5→   data only for segments actually visited, instead of pre-packing everything. *)
     6→type _ Effect.t +=
     7→  | Fetch_object_data : int -> int array Effect.t
     8→  | Fetch_collision_table : int array Effect.t
     9→
    10→type vec3 = int * int * int
    11→
    12→let ij_table = [| [| 2; 1 |]; [| 0; 2 |]; [| 1; 0 |] |]
    13→let it_none = 0
    14→let it_face = 1
    15→let it_edge = 2
    16→let it_point = 3
    17→
    18→(* Wrap native int to signed 32-bit range, matching C int32_t overflow. *)
    19→let wrap32 x = Ox_math.wrap_i64_to_fix (Int64.of_int x)
    20→
    21→(* Overflow guard: returns true if abs(a)*abs(b) would overflow bit 47+. *)
    22→let oflow_check ~a ~b =
    23→  let tmp = Int64.(abs (of_int a) * abs (of_int b)) in
    24→  Int64.(shift_right tmp 47 <> 0L)
    25→;;
    26→
    27→(* Find where line p0->p1 intersects plane (plane_pnt, plane_norm),
    28→   accounting for radius offset. Returns Some newp if found, None if parallel/no-hit. *)
    29→let find_plane_line_intersection ~plane_pnt ~plane_norm ~p0 ~p1 ~rad =
    30→  let open Ox_math in
    31→  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
    32→  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:p0 ~b:plane_pnt in
    33→  let num_raw = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:plane_norm ~b:w in
    34→  let den = wrap32 (-vm_vec_dotprod ~a:plane_norm ~b:d) in
    35→  let num = wrap32 (num_raw - rad) in
    36→  (* Check for various bad values — C operator precedence:
    37→     (den == 0) || ((den > 0) && ((num > den) || ((-num >> 15) >= den)))
    38→                 || (den < 0 && num < den) *)
    39→  let neg_num = wrap32 (-num) in
    40→  if den = 0
    41→  then None
    42→  else if den > 0 && (num > den || neg_num asr 15 >= den)
    43→  then None
    44→  else if den < 0 && num < den
    45→  then None
    46→  else (
    47→    let f1_0 = 0x10000 in
    48→    if abs num / (f1_0 / 2) >= abs den
    49→    then None (* would overflow *)
    50→    else (
    51→      let k = fixdiv ~a:num ~b:den in
    52→      let dx, dy, dz = d in
    53→      if oflow_check ~a:dx ~b:k || oflow_check ~a:dy ~b:k || oflow_check ~a:dz ~b:k
    54→      then None
    55→      else (
    56→        let scaled = vm_vec_scale2 ~v:d ~n:num ~d:den in
    57→        let newp = vm_vec_add ~a:p0 ~b:scaled in
    58→        Some (k, newp))))
    59→;;
    60→
    61→(* 2D point-in-polygon test. Projects face onto a 2D plane using ij_table
    62→   based on the largest component of the normal. Returns an edgemask where
    63→   bit N is set if the point is outside edge N. *)
    64→let check_point_to_face ~checkp ~norm ~nv ~(vertex_positions : vec3 array) =
    65→  let open Ox_math in
    66→  let cpx, cpy, cpz = checkp in
    67→  let cp = [| cpx; cpy; cpz |] in
    68→  let nx, ny, nz = norm in
    69→  let n = [| nx; ny; nz |] in
    70→  let tx = abs nx in
    71→  let ty = abs ny in
    72→  let tz = abs nz in
    73→  let biggest = if tx > ty then if tx > tz then 0 else 2 else if ty > tz then 1 else 2 in
    74→  let i, j =
    75→    if n.(biggest) > 0
    76→    then ij_table.(biggest).(0), ij_table.(biggest).(1)
    77→    else ij_table.(biggest).(1), ij_table.(biggest).(0)
    78→  in
    79→  let check_i = cp.(i) in
    80→  let check_j = cp.(j) in
    81→  let edgemask = ref 0 in
    82→  for edge = 0 to nv - 1 do
    83→    let vx0, vy0, vz0 = vertex_positions.(edge) in
    84→    let v0 = [| vx0; vy0; vz0 |] in
    85→    let vx1, vy1, vz1 = vertex_positions.((edge + 1) mod nv) in
    86→    let v1 = [| vx1; vy1; vz1 |] in
    87→    let edge_i = wrap32 (v1.(i) - v0.(i)) in
    88→    let edge_j = wrap32 (v1.(j) - v0.(j)) in
    89→    let chk_i = wrap32 (check_i - v0.(i)) in
    90→    let chk_j = wrap32 (check_j - v0.(j)) in
    91→    let d = wrap32 (fixmul ~a:chk_i ~b:edge_j - fixmul ~a:chk_j ~b:edge_i) in
    92→    if d < 0 then edgemask := !edgemask lor (1 lsl edge)
    93→  done;
    94→  !edgemask
    95→;;
    96→
    97→(* Check if a sphere (center pnt, radius rad) intersects a face.
    98→   Returns IT_FACE, IT_EDGE, IT_POINT, or IT_NONE. *)
    99→let check_sphere_to_face ~pnt ~norm ~nv ~rad ~(vertex_positions : vec3 array) =
   100→  let open Ox_math in
   101→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:pnt ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions in
   102→  if edgemask = 0
   103→  then it_face
   104→  else (
   105→    (* Find first set bit *)
   106→    let em = ref edgemask in
   107→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   108→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   109→      em := !em asr 1;
   110→      incr edgenum
   111→    done;
   112→    let v0 = vertex_positions.(!edgenum) in
   113→    let v1 = vertex_positions.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   114→    let checkvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:pnt ~b:v0 in
   115→    let edgelen, edgevec = vm_vec_normalized_dir ~v_end:v1 ~v_start:v0 in
   116→    let d = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:edgevec ~b:checkvec in
   117→    if wrap32 (d + rad) < 0
   118→    then it_none
   119→    else if wrap32 (d - rad) > edgelen
   120→    then it_none
   121→    else (
   122→      let itype = ref it_point in
   123→      let closest_point =
   124→        if d < 0
   125→        then v0
   126→        else if d > edgelen
   127→        then v1
   128→        else (
   129→          itype := it_edge;
   130→          vm_vec_scale_add ~a:v0 ~b:edgevec ~k:d)
   131→      in
   132→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:pnt ~b:closest_point in
   133→      if dist <= rad then if !itype = it_point then it_none else !itype else it_none))
   134→;;
   135→
   136→(* 3x3 matrix determinant — all intermediate sums wrap to 32-bit like C *)
   137→let calc_det_value ~rvec:(rx, ry, rz) ~uvec:(ux, uy, uz) ~fvec:(fx, fy, fz) =
   138→  let open Ox_math in
   139→  let a = fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fz) in
   140→  let b = wrap32 (a - fixmul ~a:rx ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fy)) in
   141→  let c = wrap32 (b - fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fz)) in
   142→  let d = wrap32 (c + fixmul ~a:ry ~b:(fixmul ~a:uz ~b:fx)) in
   143→  let e = wrap32 (d + fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:ux ~b:fy)) in
   144→  wrap32 (e - fixmul ~a:rz ~b:(fixmul ~a:uy ~b:fx))
   145→;;
   146→
   147→(* Line-line closest approach. Returns (ok, t1, t2).
   148→   Lines: p1 + t*v1 and p2 + t*v2. *)
   149→let check_line_to_line ~p1 ~v1 ~p2 ~v2 =
   150→  let open Ox_math in
   151→  let rvec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p2 ~b:p1 in
   152→  let fvec = vm_vec_crossprod ~a:v1 ~b:v2 in
   153→  let cross_mag2 = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:fvec ~b:fvec in
   154→  if cross_mag2 = 0
   155→  then false, 0, 0 (* parallel *)
   156→  else (
   157→    let d1 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v2 ~fvec in
   158→    if oflow_check ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2
   159→    then false, 0, 0
   160→    else (
   161→      let t1 = fixdiv ~a:d1 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   162→      let d2 = calc_det_value ~rvec ~uvec:v1 ~fvec in
   163→      if oflow_check ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2
   164→      then false, 0, 0
   165→      else (
   166→        let t2 = fixdiv ~a:d2 ~b:cross_mag2 in
   167→        true, t1, t2)))
   168→;;
   169→
   170→(* Check if line p0->p1 intersects a face. Returns (hit_type, newp).
   171→   vertex_list is the 6-element vertex index list from create_abs_vertex_lists.
   172→   facenum selects which face (0 or 1 for triangulated sides).
   173→   norm is the face normal for this facenum.
   174→   seg_verts/seg_vert_positions supply vertex position lookup. *)
   175→let check_line_to_face
   176→      ~p0
   177→      ~p1
   178→      ~norm
   179→      ~rad
   180→      ~facenum
   181→      ~nv
   182→      ~num_faces
   183→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   184→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   185→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   186→  =
   187→  let open Ox_math in
   188→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   189→  (* Use lowest point number as plane point *)
   190→  let vertnum =
   191→    if num_faces = 2
   192→    then min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2)
   193→    else (
   194→      let v = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   195→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   196→        if vertex_list.(i) < !v then v := vertex_list.(i)
   197→      done;
   198→      !v)
   199→  in
   200→  let plane_pnt = vpos vertnum in
   201→  match find_plane_line_intersection ~plane_pnt ~plane_norm:norm ~p0 ~p1 ~rad with
   202→  | None -> it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   203→  | Some (_k, newp) ->
   204→    let checkp =
   205→      if rad <> 0 then vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:newp ~src:norm ~k:(-rad) else newp
   206→    in
   207→    (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   208→    let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   209→    let hit =
   210→      check_sphere_to_face ~pnt:checkp ~norm ~nv ~rad ~vertex_positions:face_verts
   211→    in
   212→    hit, newp
   213→;;
   214→
   215→(* special_check_line_to_face: handles the case where both start and end
   216→   positions poke through the plane of a side. *)
   217→let special_check_line_to_face
   218→      ~p0
   219→      ~p1
   220→      ~norm
   221→      ~rad
   222→      ~facenum
   223→      ~nv
   224→      ~num_faces
   225→      ~side_type:_
   226→      ~sidenum:_
   227→      ~(vertex_list : int array)
   228→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   229→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   230→  =
   231→  let open Ox_math in
   232→  let vpos abs_idx = Ox_gameseg.lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   233→  (* Build vertex positions for this face *)
   234→  let face_verts = Array.init nv ~f:(fun i -> vpos vertex_list.((facenum * 3) + i)) in
   235→  (* Figure out which edge(s) to check against *)
   236→  let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:p0 ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions:face_verts in
   237→  if edgemask = 0
   238→  then
   239→    (* Start point is inside face — fall back to check_line_to_face *)
   240→    check_line_to_face
   241→      ~p0
   242→      ~p1
   243→      ~norm
   244→      ~rad
   245→      ~facenum
   246→      ~nv
   247→      ~num_faces
   248→      ~vertex_list
   249→      ~seg_verts
   250→      ~seg_vert_positions
   251→  else (
   252→    let em = ref edgemask in
   253→    let edgenum = ref 0 in
   254→    while !em land 1 = 0 do
   255→      em := !em asr 1;
   256→      incr edgenum
   257→    done;
   258→    let edge_v0 = face_verts.(!edgenum) in
   259→    let edge_v1 = face_verts.((!edgenum + 1) mod nv) in
   260→    let edge_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:edge_v1 ~b:edge_v0 in
   261→    let move_vec = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   262→    let edge_len, edge_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:edge_vec in
   263→    let move_len, move_vec_n = vm_vec_normalize ~v:move_vec in
   264→    let ok, edge_t, move_t =
   265→      check_line_to_line ~p1:edge_v0 ~v1:edge_vec_n ~p2:p0 ~v2:move_vec_n
   266→    in
   267→    if not ok
   268→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   269→    else if move_t < 0 || move_t > wrap32 (move_len + rad)
   270→    then it_none, (0, 0, 0)
   271→    else (
   272→      let move_t2 = if move_t > move_len then move_len else move_t in
   273→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t < 0 then 0 else edge_t in
   274→      let edge_t2 = if edge_t2 > edge_len then edge_len else edge_t2 in
   275→      let closest_point_edge = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:edge_v0 ~b:edge_vec_n ~k:edge_t2 in
   276→      let closest_point_move = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:move_t2 in
   277→      let closest_dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point_edge ~b:closest_point_move in
   278→      if closest_dist < wrap32 (rad * 15) / 20
   279→      then (
   280→        let newp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:move_vec_n ~k:(wrap32 (move_t - rad)) in
   281→        it_edge, newp)
   282→      else it_none, (0, 0, 0)))
   283→;;
   284→
   285→(* Ray-sphere intersection. Returns (dist, intp) where dist=0 means no hit. *)
   286→let check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos ~sphere_rad =
   287→  let open Ox_math in
   288→  let d = vm_vec_sub ~a:p1 ~b:p0 in
   289→  let w = vm_vec_sub ~a:sphere_pos ~b:p0 in
   290→  let mag_d, dn = vm_vec_copy_normalize ~v:d in
   291→  if mag_d = 0
   292→  then (
   293→    let int_dist = vm_vec_mag ~v:w in
   294→    if int_dist < sphere_rad then int_dist, p0 else 0, p0)
   295→  else (
   296→    let w_dist = vm_vec_dotprod ~a:dn ~b:w in
   297→    if w_dist < 0
   298→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* moving away *)
   299→    else if w_dist > wrap32 (mag_d + sphere_rad)
   300→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* cannot hit *)

300→    then 0, (0, 0, 0) (* cannot hit *)
   301→    else (
   302→      let closest_point = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:w_dist in
   303→      let dist = vm_vec_dist ~a:closest_point ~b:sphere_pos in
   304→      if dist < sphere_rad
   305→      then (
   306→        let dist2 = fixmul ~a:dist ~b:dist in
   307→        let rad2 = fixmul ~a:sphere_rad ~b:sphere_rad in
   308→        let shorten = fix_sqrt ~a:(wrap32 (rad2 - dist2)) in
   309→        let int_dist = wrap32 (w_dist - shorten) in
   310→        if int_dist > mag_d || int_dist < 0
   311→        then 1, p0 (* inside sphere *)
   312→        else (
   313→          let intp = vm_vec_scale_add ~a:p0 ~b:dn ~k:int_dist in
   314→          int_dist, intp))
   315→      else 0, (0, 0, 0)))
   316→;;
   317→
   318→(* Object type constants matching object.h *)
   319→let obj_robot = 2
   320→let obj_player = 4
   321→let obj_weapon = 5
   322→
   323→(* Check vector-to-object intersection with adjusted collision radius.
   324→   C original: fvi.cpp check_vector_to_object (D1+D2, identical)
   325→
   326→   Adjusts the target object's size based on type:
   327→   - Melee robots (attack_type != 0): size *= 3/4
   328→   - Players bumping into other players or coop weapon: size /= 2
   329→
   330→   Inputs:
   331→     p0, p1          - line segment endpoints (vec3)
   332→     rad              - collision radius from the moving object
   333→     obj_pos          - target object position (vec3)
   334→     obj_size         - target object size (fix)
   335→     obj_type         - target object type (OBJ_ROBOT, OBJ_PLAYER, etc.)
   336→     attack_type      - Robot_info[obj->id].attack_type (0 if not robot)
   337→     otherobj_type    - other object's type
   338→     game_mode_coop   - true if (Game_mode & GM_MULTI_COOP)
   339→     otherobj_parent_type - otherobj->ctype.laser_info.parent_type
   340→
   341→   Returns: (distance, intersection_point) — 0 distance means no hit *)
   342→let check_vector_to_object
   343→      ~p0
   344→      ~p1
   345→      ~rad
   346→      ~obj_pos
   347→      ~obj_size
   348→      ~obj_type
   349→      ~attack_type
   350→      ~otherobj_type
   351→      ~game_mode_coop
   352→      ~otherobj_parent_type
   353→  =
   354→  let size = ref obj_size in
   355→  if obj_type = obj_robot && attack_type <> 0 then size := !size * 3 / 4;
   356→  if
   357→    obj_type = obj_player
   358→    && (otherobj_type = obj_player
   359→        || (game_mode_coop
   360→            && otherobj_type = obj_weapon
   361→            && otherobj_parent_type = obj_player))
   362→  then size := !size / 2;
   363→  check_vector_to_sphere_1 ~p0 ~p1 ~sphere_pos:obj_pos ~sphere_rad:(!size + rad)
   364→;;
   365→
   366→(* ── find_vector_intersection / fvi_sub ───────────────── *)
   367→
   368→(* Constants *)
   369→let hit_none = 0
   370→let hit_wall = 1
   371→let hit_object = 2
   372→let hit_bad_p0 = 3
   373→let max_fvi_segs = 100
   374→let max_segs_visited = 100
   375→let obj_none = 255
   376→let obj_powerup = 7
   377→let max_object_types = 16
   378→
   379→(* FQ flags *)
   380→let fq_check_objs = 1
   381→let fq_transwall = 2
   382→let fq_transpoint = 4
   383→let fq_get_seglist = 8
   384→
   385→(* Callback to C's check_trans_wall for FQ_TRANSPOINT pixel-level check.
   386→   Set by fvi_bridge.ml at init time.
   387→   Args: segnum, sidenum, facenum, hit_x, hit_y, hit_z → 1 if passable *)
   388→let check_trans_wall_callback : (int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int) ref =
   389→  ref (fun _segnum _sidenum _facenum _hx _hy _hz -> 0)
   390→;;
   391→
   392→let fq_ignore_powerups = 16
   393→
   394→(* WID flags *)
   395→let wid_fly_flag = 1
   396→let wid_render_flag = 2
   397→let wid_rendpast_flag = 4
   398→let wid_transparent_wall_d1 = 6 (* D1: equality check *)
   399→
   400→(* Collision result *)
   401→let result_nothing = 0
   402→(* Mutable state threaded through fvi_sub *)
   403→type fvi_state =
   404→  { mutable n_segs_visited : int
   405→  ; segs_visited : int array
   406→  ; mutable fvi_hit_object : int
   407→  ; mutable fvi_hit_seg : int
   408→  ; mutable fvi_hit_side : int
   409→  ; mutable fvi_hit_side_seg : int
   410→  ; mutable wall_norm : vec3
   411→  ; mutable fvi_hit_seg2 : int
   412→  ; mutable fvi_nest_count : int
   413→  }
   414→
   415→let make_fvi_state () =
   416→  { n_segs_visited = 0
   417→  ; segs_visited = Array.create ~len:max_segs_visited 0
   418→  ; fvi_hit_object = -1
   419→  ; fvi_hit_seg = -1
   420→  ; fvi_hit_side = -1
   421→  ; fvi_hit_side_seg = -1
   422→  ; wall_norm = 0, 0, 0
   423→  ; fvi_hit_seg2 = -1
   424→  ; fvi_nest_count = 0
   425→  }
   426→;;
   427→
   428→(* Unpack a segment block from the packed array *)
   429→let unpack_seg (arr : int array) ~seg_base =
   430→  let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + i)) in
   431→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + 6 + i)) in
   432→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + 12 + i)) in
   433→  let normals =
   434→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   435→      let off = seg_base + 20 + (i * 3) in
   436→      arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   437→  in
   438→  let seg_vert_positions =
   439→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   440→      let off = seg_base + 56 + (i * 3) in
   441→      arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   442→  in
   443→  let wid = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(seg_base + 80 + i)) in
   444→  let first_object = arr.(seg_base + 86) in
   445→  children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions, wid, first_object
   446→;;
   447→
   448→(* Unpack an object block *)
   449→let unpack_obj (arr : int array) ~obj_base =
   450→  let obj_type = arr.(obj_base) in
   451→  let obj_id = arr.(obj_base + 1) in
   452→  let obj_flags = arr.(obj_base + 2) in
   453→  let obj_pos = arr.(obj_base + 3), arr.(obj_base + 4), arr.(obj_base + 5) in
   454→  let obj_size = arr.(obj_base + 6) in
   455→  let next_in_seg = arr.(obj_base + 7) in
   456→  let parent_type = arr.(obj_base + 8) in
   457→  let parent_num = arr.(obj_base + 9) in
   458→  let parent_sig = arr.(obj_base + 10) in
   459→  let creation_time = arr.(obj_base + 11) in
   460→  let signature = arr.(obj_base + 12) in
   461→  let attack_type = arr.(obj_base + 13) in
   462→  ( obj_type
   463→  , obj_id
   464→  , obj_flags
   465→  , obj_pos
   466→  , obj_size
   467→  , next_in_seg
   468→  , parent_type
   469→  , parent_num
   470→  , parent_sig
   471→  , creation_time
   472→  , signature
   473→  , attack_type )
   474→;;
   475→
   476→(* FVI header offsets — used by find_vector_intersection_v2 which still
   477→   receives the 18-int header from C's find_vector_intersection callsite *)
   478→let h_p0x = 0
   479→let h_p0y = 1
   480→let h_p0z = 2
   481→let h_startseg = 3
   482→let h_p1x = 4
   483→let h_p1y = 5
   484→let h_p1z = 6
   485→let h_rad = 7
   486→let h_thisobjnum = 8
   487→let h_flags = 9
   488→let h_n_segments = 10
   489→let h_n_objects = 11
   490→let h_player_objnum = 12
   491→let h_physics_cheat = 13
   492→let h_game_mode_coop = 14
   493→let h_game_time = 15
   494→let h_is_d2 = 16
   495→let h_ignore_obj_count = 17
   496→let h_ignore_obj_list = 18
   497→
   498→let obj_in_ignore_list_v2 ~(ignore_obj_list : int array) ~objnum =
   499→  let len = Array.length ignore_obj_list in
   500→  let rec loop i =
   501→    if i >= len then false
   502→    else if ignore_obj_list.(i) = objnum then true
   503→    else loop (i + 1)
   504→  in
   505→  loop 0
   506→;;
   507→
   508→(* check_laser_related using pre-fetched object data arrays *)
   509→let check_laser_related_v2 ~(obj_a : int array) ~objnum_a ~(obj_b : int array) ~objnum_b ~game_time ~is_d2 =
   510→  let packed =
   511→    [| objnum_a; obj_a.(0); obj_a.(1); obj_a.(12); obj_a.(9); obj_a.(10); obj_a.(11)
   512→     ; objnum_b; obj_b.(0); obj_b.(1); obj_b.(12); obj_b.(9); obj_b.(10); obj_b.(11)
   513→     ; game_time
   514→    |]
   515→  in
   516→  if is_d2 then Ox_collide.laser_are_related_d2 packed <> 0
   517→  else Ox_collide.laser_are_related_d1 packed <> 0
   518→;;
   519→
   520→(* Get collision result from a pre-fetched 256-element collision table *)
   521→let get_collision_result_v2 ~(collision_table : int array) ~type_a ~type_b =
   522→  if type_a >= max_object_types || type_b >= max_object_types
   523→     || type_a < 0 || type_b < 0
   524→  then result_nothing
   525→  else collision_table.(type_a * 16 + type_b)
   526→;;
   527→
   528→(* fvi_sub_v2: recursive FVI traversal using on-demand effects.
   529→   Fetches segment/object data via Fetch_segment_data / Fetch_object_data effects
   530→   instead of reading from a pre-packed array. *)
   531→let rec fvi_sub_v2
   532→          ~(collision_table : int array)
   533→          ~n_objects
   534→          ~is_d2
   535→          ~game_time
   536→          ~(ignore_obj_list : int array)
   537→          ~game_mode_coop
   538→          ~player_objnum
   539→          ~physics_cheat
   540→          (st : fvi_state)
   541→          ~p0
   542→          ~startseg
   543→          ~p1
   544→          ~rad
   545→          ~thisobjnum
   546→          ~flags
   547→          ~entry_seg
   548→  =
   549→  let open Ox_math in
   550→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data startseg) in
   551→  let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions, wid, first_object =
   552→    unpack_seg seg_data ~seg_base:0
   553→  in
   554→  let closest_d = ref 0x7fffffff in
   555→  let closest_hit_point = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   556→  let hit_type = ref hit_none in
   557→  let hit_seg = ref (-1) in
   558→  let hit_none_seg = ref (-1) in
   559→  let hit_none_n_segs = ref 0 in
   560→  let hit_none_seglist = Array.create ~len:max_fvi_segs 0 in
   561→  let cur_nest_level = st.fvi_nest_count in
   562→  let seglist = Array.create ~len:max_fvi_segs 0 in
   563→  let n_segs = ref 1 in
   564→  if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0 then seglist.(0) <- startseg;
   565→  st.fvi_nest_count <- st.fvi_nest_count + 1;
   566→  let quit = ref false in
   567→  (* Fetch thisobjnum data once for reuse *)
   568→  let thisobj_data =
   569→    if thisobjnum > -1 && thisobjnum < n_objects
   570→    then Some (Effect.perform (Fetch_object_data thisobjnum))
   571→    else None
   572→  in
   573→  let thisobj_type = match thisobj_data with Some d -> d.(0) | None -> 0 in
   574→  let thisobj_attack = match thisobj_data with Some d -> d.(13) | None -> 0 in
   575→  (* Check objects in this segment *)
   576→  if flags land fq_check_objs <> 0 && not !quit
   577→  then (
   578→    let objnum = ref first_object in
   579→    while !objnum <> -1 && not !quit do
   580→      let ob = !objnum in
   581→      if ob < 0 || ob >= n_objects then
   582→        objnum := -1
   583→      else
   584→      let ob_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_data ob) in
   585→      let ( obj_type_ob
   586→          , _obj_id_ob
   587→          , obj_flags_ob
   588→          , obj_pos_ob
   589→          , obj_size_ob
   590→          , next_in_seg
   591→          , parent_type_ob
   592→          , _parent_num_ob
   593→          , _parent_sig_ob
   594→          , _creation_time_ob
   595→          , _signature_ob
   596→          , attack_type_ob )
   597→        =
   598→        unpack_obj ob_data ~obj_base:0
   599→      in

600→      let skip = ref false in
   601→      if obj_type_ob = obj_none then skip := true;
   602→      if obj_flags_ob land 2 <> 0 then skip := true;
   603→      if thisobjnum = ob then skip := true;
   604→      if (not !skip) && obj_in_ignore_list_v2 ~ignore_obj_list ~objnum:ob then skip := true;
   605→      if not !skip
   606→      then (
   607→        match thisobj_data with
   608→        | Some td ->
   609→          if check_laser_related_v2
   610→               ~obj_a:ob_data ~objnum_a:ob
   611→               ~obj_b:td ~objnum_b:thisobjnum
   612→               ~game_time ~is_d2
   613→          then skip := true
   614→        | None -> ());
   615→      if (not !skip) && thisobjnum > -1
   616→      then (
   617→        let cr1 =
   618→          get_collision_result_v2 ~collision_table ~type_a:thisobj_type ~type_b:obj_type_ob
   619→        in
   620→        let cr2 =
   621→          get_collision_result_v2 ~collision_table ~type_a:obj_type_ob ~type_b:thisobj_type
   622→        in
   623→        if cr1 = result_nothing && cr2 = result_nothing then skip := true);
   624→      if
   625→        (not !skip)
   626→        && is_d2
   627→        && obj_type_ob = obj_powerup
   628→        && flags land fq_ignore_powerups <> 0
   629→      then skip := true;
   630→      if (not !skip) && thisobjnum > -1
   631→      then (
   632→        if thisobj_type = obj_robot && obj_type_ob = obj_robot
   633→        then
   634→          if is_d2
   635→          then skip := true
   636→          else if not (attack_type_ob <> 0 && thisobj_attack <> 0)
   637→          then skip := true);
   638→      if not !skip
   639→      then (
   640→        let fudged_rad = ref rad in
   641→        if thisobjnum > -1
   642→        then (
   643→          if thisobj_type = obj_robot && thisobj_attack <> 0
   644→          then fudged_rad := rad * 3 / 4;
   645→          if
   646→            thisobj_type = obj_player
   647→            && (obj_type_ob = obj_player
   648→                || (game_mode_coop
   649→                    && obj_type_ob = obj_weapon
   650→                    && parent_type_ob = obj_player))
   651→          then fudged_rad := rad / 2);
   652→        let d, hit_point =
   653→          check_vector_to_object
   654→            ~p0
   655→            ~p1
   656→            ~rad:!fudged_rad
   657→            ~obj_pos:obj_pos_ob
   658→            ~obj_size:obj_size_ob
   659→            ~obj_type:obj_type_ob
   660→            ~attack_type:attack_type_ob
   661→            ~otherobj_type:thisobj_type
   662→            ~game_mode_coop
   663→            ~otherobj_parent_type:
   664→              (match thisobj_data with Some d -> d.(8) | None -> 0)
   665→        in
   666→        if d <> 0 && d < !closest_d
   667→        then (
   668→          st.fvi_hit_object <- ob;
   669→          closest_d := d;
   670→          closest_hit_point := hit_point;
   671→          hit_type := hit_object));
   672→      objnum := next_in_seg
   673→    done);
   674→  (* HACK: if this object type doesn't collide with walls, set rad=0 *)
   675→  let rad =
   676→    if thisobjnum > -1
   677→    then (
   678→      let cr =
   679→        get_collision_result_v2 ~collision_table ~type_a:thisobj_type ~type_b:0
   680→      in
   681→      if cr = result_nothing then 0 else rad)
   682→    else rad
   683→  in
   684→  (* Get start/end masks *)
   685→  let startmask =
   686→    let fm, _, _ =
   687→      Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   688→        ~checkp:p0 ~rad ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   689→    in
   690→    fm
   691→  in
   692→  let endmask, _sidemask, centermask =
   693→    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   694→      ~checkp:p1 ~rad ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   695→  in
   696→  if centermask = 0 then hit_none_seg := startseg;
   697→  if endmask <> 0 && not !quit
   698→  then (
   699→    let bit = ref 1 in
   700→    let side = ref 0 in
   701→    while !side < 6 && endmask >= !bit && not !quit do
   702→      let sn = !side in
   703→      let num_faces_raw =
   704→        let st = side_types.(sn) in
   705→        if st = 1 then 1 else if st = 2 || st = 3 then 2 else 1
   706→      in
   707→      let num_faces = if num_faces_raw = 0 then 1 else num_faces_raw in
   708→      for face = 0 to 1 do
   709→        if (not !quit) && endmask land !bit <> 0
   710→        then
   711→          if children.(sn) <> entry_seg
   712→          then (
   713→            let nv = if num_faces = 1 then 4 else 3 in
   714→            let _nf, vertex_list =
   715→              Ox_gameseg.create_abs_vertex_lists
   716→                ~side_type:side_types.(sn) ~seg_verts ~sidenum:sn
   717→            in
   718→            let norm = normals.((sn * 2) + face) in
   719→            let face_hit_type, hit_point =
   720→              if startmask land !bit <> 0
   721→              then
   722→                special_check_line_to_face
   723→                  ~p0 ~p1 ~norm ~rad ~facenum:face ~nv ~num_faces
   724→                  ~side_type:side_types.(sn) ~sidenum:sn
   725→                  ~vertex_list ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions
   726→              else
   727→                check_line_to_face
   728→                  ~p0 ~p1 ~norm ~rad ~facenum:face ~nv ~num_faces
   729→                  ~vertex_list ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions
   730→            in
   731→            if face_hit_type <> 0
   732→            then (
   733→              let wid_flag =
   734→                let base_wid = wid.(sn) in
   735→                if thisobjnum = player_objnum && physics_cheat
   736→                then (
   737→                  let w = base_wid in
   738→                  if children.(sn) >= 0 then w lor wid_fly_flag else w)
   739→                else base_wid
   740→              in
   741→              let is_flyable = wid_flag land wid_fly_flag <> 0 in
   742→              let is_trans =
   743→                if is_d2
   744→                then
   745→                  wid_flag land wid_render_flag <> 0
   746→                  && wid_flag land wid_rendpast_flag <> 0
   747→                else wid_flag = wid_transparent_wall_d1
   748→              in
   749→              let passable =
   750→                is_flyable
   751→                || (is_trans && flags land fq_transwall <> 0)
   752→                || (is_trans
   753→                    && flags land fq_transpoint <> 0
   754→                    &&
   755→                    let hx, hy, hz = hit_point in
   756→                    !check_trans_wall_callback startseg sn face hx hy hz <> 0)
   757→              in
   758→              if passable
   759→              then (
   760→                let newsegnum = children.(sn) in
   761→                let already_visited = ref false in
   762→                for i = 0 to st.n_segs_visited - 1 do
   763→                  if st.segs_visited.(i) = newsegnum then already_visited := true
   764→                done;
   765→                if not !already_visited
   766→                then (
   767→                  st.segs_visited.(st.n_segs_visited) <- newsegnum;
   768→                  st.n_segs_visited <- st.n_segs_visited + 1;
   769→                  if st.n_segs_visited >= max_segs_visited
   770→                  then quit := true
   771→                  else (
   772→                    let save_wall_norm = st.wall_norm in
   773→                    let save_hit_objnum = st.fvi_hit_object in
   774→                    let ( sub_hit_type
   775→                        , sub_hit_point
   776→                        , sub_hit_seg
   777→                        , temp_seglist
   778→                        , temp_n_segs )
   779→                      =
   780→                      fvi_sub_v2
   781→                        ~collision_table ~n_objects ~is_d2 ~game_time
   782→                        ~ignore_obj_list ~game_mode_coop ~player_objnum ~physics_cheat
   783→                        st
   784→                        ~p0 ~startseg:newsegnum ~p1 ~rad ~thisobjnum ~flags
   785→                        ~entry_seg:startseg
   786→                    in
   787→                    if sub_hit_type <> hit_none
   788→                    then (
   789→                      let d = vm_vec_dist ~a:sub_hit_point ~b:p0 in
   790→                      if d < !closest_d
   791→                      then (
   792→                        closest_d := d;
   793→                        closest_hit_point := sub_hit_point;
   794→                        hit_type := sub_hit_type;
   795→                        if sub_hit_seg <> -1 then hit_seg := sub_hit_seg;
   796→                        if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
   797→                        then (
   798→                          let ii = ref 0 in
   799→                          while !ii < temp_n_segs && !n_segs < max_fvi_segs - 1 do
   800→                            seglist.(!n_segs) <- temp_seglist.(!ii);
   801→                            n_segs := !n_segs + 1;
   802→                            ii := !ii + 1
   803→                          done))
   804→                      else (
   805→                        st.wall_norm <- save_wall_norm;
   806→                        st.fvi_hit_object <- save_hit_objnum))
   807→                    else (
   808→                      st.wall_norm <- save_wall_norm;
   809→                      if sub_hit_seg <> -1 then hit_none_seg := sub_hit_seg;
   810→                      if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
   811→                      then
   812→                        for ii = 0 to min (temp_n_segs - 1) (max_fvi_segs - 2) do
   813→                          hit_none_seglist.(ii) <- temp_seglist.(ii)
   814→                        done;
   815→                      hit_none_n_segs := temp_n_segs))))
   816→              else (
   817→                let d = vm_vec_dist ~a:hit_point ~b:p0 in
   818→                if d < !closest_d
   819→                then (
   820→                  closest_d := d;
   821→                  closest_hit_point := hit_point;
   822→                  hit_type := hit_wall;
   823→                  st.wall_norm <- normals.((sn * 2) + face);
   824→                  let _, _, cm =
   825→                    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   826→                      ~checkp:hit_point ~rad ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   827→                  in
   828→                  if cm = 0 then hit_seg := startseg else st.fvi_hit_seg2 <- startseg;
   829→                  st.fvi_hit_seg <- !hit_seg;
   830→                  st.fvi_hit_side <- sn;
   831→                  st.fvi_hit_side_seg <- startseg))));
   832→        bit := !bit lsl 1
   833→      done;
   834→      incr side
   835→    done);
   836→  if !hit_type = hit_none
   837→  then (
   838→    let intp = p1 in
   839→    let ints = !hit_none_seg in
   840→    if !hit_none_seg <> -1
   841→    then (
   842→      if flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
   843→      then
   844→        for i = 0 to !hit_none_n_segs - 1 do
   845→          if !n_segs < max_fvi_segs - 1
   846→          then (
   847→            seglist.(!n_segs) <- hit_none_seglist.(i);
   848→            n_segs := !n_segs + 1)
   849→        done)
   850→    else if cur_nest_level <> 0
   851→    then n_segs := 0;
   852→    !hit_type, intp, ints, seglist, !n_segs)
   853→  else (
   854→    let intp = !closest_hit_point in
   855→    let ints =
   856→      if !hit_seg = -1
   857→      then if st.fvi_hit_seg2 <> -1 then st.fvi_hit_seg2 else !hit_none_seg
   858→      else !hit_seg
   859→    in
   860→    !hit_type, intp, ints, seglist, !n_segs)
   861→;;
   862→
   863→(* Unpack segment data for centermask check (returns just the fields needed) *)
   864→let unpack_seg_for_masks seg_data =
   865→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
   866→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
   867→  let normals =
   868→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   869→      let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
   870→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   871→  in
   872→  let seg_vert_positions =
   873→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   874→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   875→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   876→  in
   877→  seg_verts, side_types, normals, seg_vert_positions
   878→;;
   879→
   880→(* find_point_seg_fvi_v2: uses Fetch_segment_data effects *)
   881→let find_point_seg_fvi_v2 ~point:(px, py, pz) ~segnum ~n_segments =
   882→  let fetch s =
   883→    let seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data s) in
   884→    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
   885→    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
   886→    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
   887→    let normals =
   888→      Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   889→        let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
   890→        seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   891→    in
   892→    let seg_vert_positions =
   893→      Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   894→        let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   895→        seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   896→    in
   897→    children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   898→  in
   899→  let rec trace s iterations =

900→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments then -1
   901→    else if iterations > 1024 then s
   902→    else (
   903→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch s in
   904→      let centermask, side_dists =
   905→        Ox_gameseg.get_side_dists
   906→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz) ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   907→      in
   908→      if centermask = 0 then s
   909→      else (
   910→        let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   911→        let sidenum = ref (-1) in
   912→        for i = 0 to 5 do
   913→          if centermask land (1 lsl i) <> 0 && children.(i) >= 0
   914→          then
   915→            if side_dists.(i) < !biggest_val
   916→            then (
   917→              biggest_val := side_dists.(i);
   918→              sidenum := i)
   919→        done;
   920→        if !sidenum <> -1
   921→        then trace children.(!sidenum) (iterations + 1)
   922→        else -1))
   923→  in
   924→  if segnum < 0 || segnum >= n_segments then -1 else trace segnum 0
   925→;;
   926→
   927→(* find_vector_intersection_v2: top-level FVI using on-demand effects.
   928→   Takes just the header array (18+N ints), fetches data via effects. *)
   929→let find_vector_intersection_v2 (arr : int array) =
   930→  let p0 = arr.(h_p0x), arr.(h_p0y), arr.(h_p0z) in
   931→  let startseg = arr.(h_startseg) in
   932→  let p1 = arr.(h_p1x), arr.(h_p1y), arr.(h_p1z) in
   933→  let rad = arr.(h_rad) in
   934→  let thisobjnum = arr.(h_thisobjnum) in
   935→  let flags = arr.(h_flags) in
   936→  let n_segments = arr.(h_n_segments) in
   937→  let n_objects = arr.(h_n_objects) in
   938→  let player_objnum = arr.(h_player_objnum) in
   939→  let physics_cheat = arr.(h_physics_cheat) <> 0 in
   940→  let game_mode_coop = arr.(h_game_mode_coop) <> 0 in
   941→  let game_time = arr.(h_game_time) in
   942→  let is_d2 = arr.(h_is_d2) <> 0 in
   943→  let ignore_count = arr.(h_ignore_obj_count) in
   944→  let ignore_obj_list = Array.init ignore_count ~f:(fun i -> arr.(h_ignore_obj_list + i)) in
   945→  let collision_table = Effect.perform Fetch_collision_table in
   946→  (* Validate startseg *)
   947→  let start_seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data startseg) in
   948→  let seg_verts, side_types, normals, seg_vert_positions =
   949→    unpack_seg_for_masks start_seg_data
   950→  in
   951→  let _, _, centermask =
   952→    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   953→      ~checkp:p0 ~rad:0 ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
   954→  in
   955→  if centermask <> 0
   956→  then (
   957→    let p0x, p0y, p0z = p0 in
   958→    [| hit_bad_p0; p0x; p0y; p0z; startseg; 0; -1; 0; 0; 0; 0; 0 |])
   959→  else (
   960→    let st = make_fvi_state () in
   961→    st.segs_visited.(0) <- startseg;
   962→    st.n_segs_visited <- 1;
   963→    st.fvi_hit_seg2 <- -1;
   964→    let hit_type, hit_pnt, hit_seg2, seglist, n_segs =
   965→      fvi_sub_v2
   966→        ~collision_table ~n_objects ~is_d2 ~game_time ~ignore_obj_list
   967→        ~game_mode_coop ~player_objnum ~physics_cheat
   968→        st ~p0 ~startseg ~p1 ~rad ~thisobjnum ~flags ~entry_seg:(-2)
   969→    in
   970→    let hit_pnt_x = ref (let x, _, _ = hit_pnt in x) in
   971→    let hit_pnt_y = ref (let _, y, _ = hit_pnt in y) in
   972→    let hit_pnt_z = ref (let _, _, z = hit_pnt in z) in
   973→    let cur_hit_pnt () = (!hit_pnt_x, !hit_pnt_y, !hit_pnt_z) in
   974→    (* Resolve hit_seg *)
   975→    let hit_seg = ref (-1) in
   976→    if hit_seg2 <> -1
   977→    then (
   978→      let sd2 = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data hit_seg2) in
   979→      let sv2, st2, n2, svp2 = unpack_seg_for_masks sd2 in
   980→      let _, _, cm2 =
   981→        Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   982→          ~checkp:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~rad:0 ~seg_verts:sv2 ~side_types:st2 ~normals:n2 ~seg_vert_positions:svp2
   983→      in
   984→      if cm2 = 0 then hit_seg := hit_seg2);
   985→    if !hit_seg = -1
   986→    then
   987→      hit_seg := find_point_seg_fvi_v2 ~point:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~segnum:startseg ~n_segments;
   988→    (* HACK: if HIT_WALL and hit_seg=-1, try fvi_hit_seg2 *)
   989→    if hit_type = hit_wall && !hit_seg = -1 && st.fvi_hit_seg2 <> -1
   990→    then (
   991→      let sd2 = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data st.fvi_hit_seg2) in
   992→      let sv2, st2, n2, svp2 = unpack_seg_for_masks sd2 in
   993→      let _, _, cm2 =
   994→        Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
   995→          ~checkp:(cur_hit_pnt ()) ~rad:0 ~seg_verts:sv2 ~side_types:st2 ~normals:n2 ~seg_vert_positions:svp2
   996→      in
   997→      if cm2 = 0 then hit_seg := st.fvi_hit_seg2);
   998→    (* Retry with rad=0 if hit_seg still -1 *)
   999→    if !hit_seg = -1
  1000→    then (
  1001→      let st2 = make_fvi_state () in
  1002→      st2.segs_visited.(0) <- startseg;
  1003→      st2.n_segs_visited <- 1;
  1004→      st2.fvi_hit_seg2 <- -1;
  1005→      let _new_hit_type, _new_hit_pnt, new_hit_seg2, _new_seglist, _new_n_segs =
  1006→        fvi_sub_v2
  1007→          ~collision_table ~n_objects ~is_d2 ~game_time ~ignore_obj_list
  1008→          ~game_mode_coop ~player_objnum ~physics_cheat
  1009→          st2 ~p0 ~startseg ~p1 ~rad:0 ~thisobjnum ~flags ~entry_seg:(-2)
  1010→      in
  1011→      if new_hit_seg2 <> -1
  1012→      then (
  1013→        hit_seg := new_hit_seg2;
  1014→        let npx, npy, npz = _new_hit_pnt in
  1015→        hit_pnt_x := npx;
  1016→        hit_pnt_y := npy;
  1017→        hit_pnt_z := npz));
  1018→    (* Trim seglist at hit point *)
  1019→    let final_seglist = Array.create ~len:max_fvi_segs 0 in
  1020→    let final_n_segs = ref n_segs in
  1021→    Array.blit ~src:seglist ~src_pos:0 ~dst:final_seglist ~dst_pos:0 ~len:n_segs;
  1022→    if !hit_seg <> -1 && flags land fq_get_seglist <> 0
  1023→    then
  1024→      if
  1025→        !final_n_segs > 0
  1026→        && final_seglist.(!final_n_segs - 1) <> !hit_seg
  1027→        && !final_n_segs < max_fvi_segs - 1

content.text:
`fixquadadjust` shifts the 64-bit dot product right by 16 bits and clamps to `[-0x7FFFFFFF, 0x7FFFFFFF]` if there's a sign mismatch (overflow). This means `vm_vec_dotprod` already returns a value within the 32-bit signed range (clamped). So `num_raw` at OCaml line 33 is always within `[-2^31, 2^31-1]` (after `fixquadadjust`).

Similarly `den = wrap32(-vm_vec_dotprod...)` — the negation of a 32-bit clamped value. `num = wrap32(num_raw - rad)` — subtraction of two 32-bit values, wrapped to 32-bit. **This is correct behavior**, because in C `num` is already int32_t and `num -= rad` wraps naturally. No bug.

---

---

## Final Report: OCaml vs C FVI Bug Analysis

### Bug 1 (Real Divergence): `fvi_sub` seglist copy — OCaml fixes C's typo bug

**Location:** C `main_d2/fvi.cpp` line 1152 vs OCaml `ox/ox_fvi.ml` lines 798-803

**C code (buggy):**
```c
for (ii=0;i<temp_n_segs && *n_segs<MAX_FVI_SEGS-1;)
    seglist[(*n_segs)++] = temp_seglist[ii++];
```
The loop condition uses `i` (the outer `side` loop variable, currently equal to the side that caused the recursive hit) instead of `ii`. Since `i` ranges 0-5 and `temp_n_segs` is typically small (say 1-3), this loop body often runs when `i < temp_n_segs` (early sides 0, 1, 2), but stops abruptly when `i >= temp_n_segs`. This causes a **partial or zero-copy** of the seglist in the successful-sub-hit case.

**OCaml code (correct but diverges from C):**
```ocaml
let ii = ref 0 in
while !ii < temp_n_segs && !n_segs < max_fvi_segs - 1 do
  seglist.(!n_segs) <- temp_seglist.(!ii);
  ...
done
```
OCaml correctly copies all `temp_n_segs` entries. **Result: `hit_data->n_segs` and `hit_data->seglist` diverge whenever `FQ_GET_SEGLIST` is set and a passable-wall recursion produces a hit.**

---

### Bug 2 (Real Divergence): `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` is a simplified trace vs C's full DFS

**Location:** `ox/ox_fvi.ml` lines 880-924 vs C `gameseg.cpp` `trace_segs` lines 960-1004

**OCaml `find_point_seg_fvi_v2`:** Follows only the ONE best child (most-negative `side_dists`), recursing linearly.

**C `trace_segs`:** Uses a `do...while` loop that retries ALL centermask-bit sides (zeroing each after trying), performing true multi-branch DFS. If first child fails, tries second, third, etc. Falls back to exhaustive all-segment scan if trace fails entirely.

**Result:** When a hit point lies near a segment boundary and the "best single child" doesn't contain it, OCaml returns `hit_seg = -1` where C would find the correct segment. This affects `hit_data->hit_seg` reliability and can cause physics/collision responses to malfunction.

Note: The full `ox_gameseg.ml` `find_point_seg` (used when called through the bridge) correctly implements the do-while DFS (lines 782-803). Only the FVI-internal `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` is weaker.

---

### Bug 3 (Suspected, needs verification): `special_check_line_to_face` — `check_line_to_face` fallback uses wrong `num_faces`

**Location:** C `main_d2/fvi.cpp` line 407-408 vs OCaml `ox/ox_fvi.ml` lines 237-251

In C when `edgemask == 0` (start point inside face), `special_check_line_to_face` falls back to `check_line_to_face` with the **original** `num_faces` value (may be 1 or 2). But in OCaml at lines 237-251, when falling back, the call:
```ocaml
check_line_to_face ~p0 ~p1 ~norm ~rad ~facenum ~nv ~num_faces ~vertex_list ...
```
receives `num_faces` which was computed from `side_types.(sidenum)` in `fvi_sub_v2` before calling `special_check_line_to_face`. The OCaml correctly passes `num_faces` through the fallback. **This appears correct.**

---

### Bug 4 (Minor): `check_sphere_to_face` uses `pnt` not `checkp` for `edgemask`

**C** `check_sphere_to_face` (line 239): `edgemask = check_point_to_face(pnt, s, facenum, nv, vertex_list)` — uses `pnt` (the original sphere center).

**OCaml** line 101: `let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:pnt ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions in`

**But in `check_line_to_face`:** C (line 343-348) computes `checkp = *newp` then adjusts `checkp` by `−rad * norm` if `rad != 0`, then calls `check_sphere_to_face(&checkp, ...)`. But the OCaml (lines 204-210) also adjusts `checkp`, then passes `checkp` into `check_sphere_to_face` as `pnt`. So `check_sphere_to_face` in OCaml always receives the already-adjusted `checkp` (which matches C's intent). **No bug here.**

---

### Verified Correct Areas

- `find_plane_line_intersection`: condition logic, wrap32 usage, and overflow guards all match C.
- `check_point_to_face`: vertex indexing uses pre-built `face_verts` (face-offset) instead of inline `vertex_list[facenum*3+edge]`, but the offset is applied equivalently by callers.
- `check_sphere_to_face`: `IT_POINT` → `IT_NONE` behavior matches.
- `special_check_line_to_face`: edge-hit threshold `rad*15/20` (plain multiply) matches C.
- Robot-robot skip logic: D2 unconditional skip, D1 attack-type conditional — both match.
- `oflow_check`: bit-47 shift matches C exactly.
- `check_vector_to_object`: radius reductions on both sides (moving object's `rad`, target's `size`) match C.
- `sphere_intersects_wall_v2`: visited tracking and `IS_CHILD` equivalent (`child <= -1` → return 1) match.
- `calc_det_value`: accumulation order and wrap32 at each step matches C.
- `check_line_to_line`: parallel check, overflow guard, and fixdiv matches C.

---

### Priority Summary

| Priority | Bug | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| High | Seglist copy uses `i` vs `ii` in C (OCaml is correct but diverges) | `hit_data->seglist` differs when `FQ_GET_SEGLIST` is active |
| Medium | `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` missing multi-branch DFS | `hit_seg = -1` in boundary cases where C finds the correct segment |
| Low | None identified beyond the above two structural divergences | — |

The seglist divergence (Bug 1) is technically a case where OCaml is MORE correct than C, but since the goal is parity, it may need to be matched. The `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` simplification (Bug 2) is an intentional tradeoff (the exhaustive scan and do-while DFS were not ported into the FVI-internal version), and is likely responsible for some of the observed `hit_seg` divergences.

**OCaml `find_point_seg_fvi_v2`:** Follows only the ONE best child (most-negative `side_dists`), recursing linearly.

**C `trace_segs`:** Uses a `do...while` loop that retries ALL centermask-bit sides (zeroing each after trying), performing true multi-branch DFS. If first child fails, tries second, third, etc. Falls back to exhaustive all-segment scan if trace fails entirely.

**Result:** When a hit point lies near a segment boundary and the "best single child" doesn't contain it, OCaml returns `hit_seg = -1` where C would find the correct segment. This affects `hit_data->hit_seg` reliability and can cause physics/collision responses to malfunction.

Note: The full `ox_gameseg.ml` `find_point_seg` (used when called through the bridge) correctly implements the do-while DFS (lines 782-803). Only the FVI-internal `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` is weaker.

---

### Bug 3 (Suspected, needs verification): `special_check_line_to_face` — `check_line_to_face` fallback uses wrong `num_faces`

**Location:** C `main_d2/fvi.cpp` line 407-408 vs OCaml `ox/ox_fvi.ml` lines 237-251

In C when `edgemask == 0` (start point inside face), `special_check_line_to_face` falls back to `check_line_to_face` with the **original** `num_faces` value (may be 1 or 2). But in OCaml at lines 237-251, when falling back, the call:
```ocaml
check_line_to_face ~p0 ~p1 ~norm ~rad ~facenum ~nv ~num_faces ~vertex_list ...
```
receives `num_faces` which was computed from `side_types.(sidenum)` in `fvi_sub_v2` before calling `special_check_line_to_face`. The OCaml correctly passes `num_faces` through the fallback. **This appears correct.**

---

### Bug 4 (Minor): `check_sphere_to_face` uses `pnt` not `checkp` for `edgemask`

**C** `check_sphere_to_face` (line 239): `edgemask = check_point_to_face(pnt, s, facenum, nv, vertex_list)` — uses `pnt` (the original sphere center).

**OCaml** line 101: `let edgemask = check_point_to_face ~checkp:pnt ~norm ~nv ~vertex_positions in`

**But in `check_line_to_face`:** C (line 343-348) computes `checkp = *newp` then adjusts `checkp` by `−rad * norm` if `rad != 0`, then calls `check_sphere_to_face(&checkp, ...)`. But the OCaml (lines 204-210) also adjusts `checkp`, then passes `checkp` into `check_sphere_to_face` as `pnt`. So `check_sphere_to_face` in OCaml always receives the already-adjusted `checkp` (which matches C's intent). **No bug here.**

---

### Verified Correct Areas

- `find_plane_line_intersection`: condition logic, wrap32 usage, and overflow guards all match C.
- `check_point_to_face`: vertex indexing uses pre-built `face_verts` (face-offset) instead of inline `vertex_list[facenum*3+edge]`, but the offset is applied equivalently by callers.
- `check_sphere_to_face`: `IT_POINT` → `IT_NONE` behavior matches.
- `special_check_line_to_face`: edge-hit threshold `rad*15/20` (plain multiply) matches C.
- Robot-robot skip logic: D2 unconditional skip, D1 attack-type conditional — both match.
- `oflow_check`: bit-47 shift matches C exactly.
- `check_vector_to_object`: radius reductions on both sides (moving object's `rad`, target's `size`) match C.
- `sphere_intersects_wall_v2`: visited tracking and `IS_CHILD` equivalent (`child <= -1` → return 1) match.
- `calc_det_value`: accumulation order and wrap32 at each step matches C.
- `check_line_to_line`: parallel check, overflow guard, and fixdiv matches C.

---

### Priority Summary

| Priority | Bug | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| High | Seglist copy uses `i` vs `ii` in C (OCaml is correct but diverges) | `hit_data->seglist` differs when `FQ_GET_SEGLIST` is active |
| Medium | `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` missing multi-branch DFS | `hit_seg = -1` in boundary cases where C finds the correct segment |
| Low | None identified beyond the above two structural divergences | — |

The seglist divergence (Bug 1) is technically a case where OCaml is MORE correct than C, but since the goal is parity, it may need to be matched. The `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` simplification (Bug 2) is an intentional tradeoff (the exhaustive scan and do-while DFS were not ported into the FVI-internal version), and is likely responsible for some of the observed `hit_seg` divergences.

(* find_point_seg_fvi_v2: uses Fetch_segment_data effects *)
let find_point_seg_fvi_v2 ~point:(px, py, pz) ~segnum ~n_segments =
  let fetch s =
    let seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data s) in
    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
    let normals =
      Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
        let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
        seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
    in
    let seg_vert_positions =
      Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
        let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
        seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
    in
    children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
  in
  let rec trace s iterations =
    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments then -1
    else if iterations > 1024 then s
    else (
      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch s in
      let centermask, side_dists =
        Ox_gameseg.get_side_dists
          ~checkp:(px, py, pz) ~seg_verts ~side_types ~normals ~seg_vert_positions
      in
      if centermask = 0 then s
      else (
        let biggest_val = ref 0 in
        let sidenum = ref (-1) in
        for i = 0 to 5 do
          if centermask land (1 lsl i) <> 0 && children.(i) >= 0
          then
            if side_dists.(i) < !biggest_val
            then (
              biggest_val := side_dists.(i);
              sidenum := i)
        done;
        if !sidenum <> -1
        then trace children.(!sidenum) (iterations + 1)
        else -1))
  in
  if segnum < 0 || segnum >= n_segments then -1 else trace segnum 0
;;

   Takes just the header array (18+N ints), fetches data via effects. *)

content.thinking:
Now I see the key difference:

**`find_point_seg` (ox_gameseg.ml)**: Full implementation with:
1. `trace_segs` that tries ALL children when the best child fails (lines 782-803): it zeroes the side_dist of the tried child and re-loops to try the next best, implementing the multi-branch DFS
2. Exhaustive scan fallback when trace_segs fails

**`find_point_seg_fvi_v2` (ox_fvi.ml)**: Simplified version:
1. Only follows the ONE best child per iteration (lines 910-921): if the best child doesn't contain the point, returns -1


2. No multi-branch DFS  
3. No exhaustive scan fallback

This is a significant difference. When a hit point is near a segment boundary, `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` might return -1 where the full implementation would find the correct segment. In FVI, this -1 causes `iseg = -1` which is treated as a "horrible error" — the physics sim breaks out of the loop or marks the object as dead. Now I need to check if `find_point_seg_v2` has the same limitation.

let find_point_seg_v2 ~point:(px, py, pz) ~segnum ~n_segments ~doing_lighting_hack =
  let fetch s = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data s) |> unpack_seg_from_fetch in
  let rec trace s iterations =
    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments
    then -1
    else if iterations > 1024
    then s
    else (
      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch s in
      let centermask, side_dists =
        get_side_dists
          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)

content.thinking:
`find_point_seg_fvi_v2` is called in two places:
1. Line 987: FVI main loop - finding the segment of a hit point when centermask check fails2. Line 1891: `player_is_visible_from_object_v2` - finding the segment of a gun point

The simplified single-child trace in `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` could return -1 in boundary cases where the full DFS would succeed. This could cause physics/FVI differences.The fix would be to upgrade `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` to use the same multi-branch DFS as `find_point_seg_v2`. The key difference is:


Implementing the full DFS approach... I see that the C implementation does an exhaustive segment scan as a fallback, but that's not practical here since FVI uses on-demand effects and would need to fetch all segments upfront. The multi-branch DFS approach should handle most cases adequately, so I'll go with that optimization to improve segment-finding accuracy in edge cases.

content.text:
Found the issue. `find_point_seg_fvi_v2` in ox_fvi.ml uses a single-child trace (follows only the best child, gives up on failure), while C's trace_segs tries ALL children in order of distance. This can cause false -1 returns at segment boundaries, leading to FVI divergences.

Let me fix it to use the same multi-branch DFS as `find_point_seg_v2`.
